records.ety: [L. mitigatus, p. p. of mitigare to soften, mitigate; mitis mild, soft + the root of agere to do, drive.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Mitigated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Mitigating.]

defs: To make less severe, intense, harsh, rigorous, painful, etc.; to soften; to meliorate; to alleviate; to diminish; to lessen; as, to mitigate heat or cold; to mitigate grief.
To make mild and accessible; to mollify; -- applied to persons.
quotes: This opinion . . . mitigated kings into companions.
